# Norns

> Durability for AI agents. An open-source runtime that keeps agent runs alive across crashes, retries, and deploys, without ever touching your API keys or data.

From simple loops to complex workflows, Norns makes agents durable. Workers execute LLM calls and tools in your own infrastructure; the orchestrator persists run state so in-flight work survives worker crashes, resumes after deploys, and retries never produce duplicate side effects.

Core primitives: Agents (definition of a model, system prompt, tools, and mode), Runs (single execution instances), Event logs (append-only timelines of requests, results, failures, retries), Checkpoints (durable state snapshots used for resume and replay), and Workers (processes that execute LLM or tool tasks).

Licensed MIT. Version v0.x — the runtime, SDKs, and CLI are in active development; APIs are stabilizing but not yet stable.

## Project

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/nornscode/norns): Elixir orchestrator, CLI, and protocol source
- [README](https://github.com/nornscode/norns#readme): Quickstart, concepts, and primary documentation
- [OpenAPI spec](https://github.com/nornscode/norns/blob/main/openapi.yaml): HTTP API surface

## SDKs

- [Python SDK](https://github.com/amackera/norns-sdk-python): Official Python worker and client library
- [Elixir SDK](https://github.com/amackera/norns-sdk-elixir): Official Elixir worker and client library

## Tooling

- [nornsctl](https://github.com/amackera/nornsctl): Command-line tool for running a local orchestrator and scaffolding agent projects

## Examples

- [Hello agent](https://github.com/amackera/norns-hello-agent): Minimal walkthrough agent
- [Mimir](https://github.com/amackera/norns-mimir-agent): Full example application built on Norns

## Install

Install the CLI via Homebrew, then start a local orchestrator and scaffold an agent project:

```
brew install amackera/tap/nornsctl
nornsctl dev
nornsctl new my-agent && cd my-agent
uv sync && uv run my-agent-worker
```

## Optional

- [MIT License](https://github.com/nornscode/norns/blob/main/LICENSE)
